note: Curated illustrative peer sets per sector for the benchmarking view — small, recently-listed or mid-cap French/European names whose approximate EV/EBITDA and EV/Revenue give an SME a sense of where comparable businesses trade. These are INDICATIVE reference points for orientation, drawn from public secondary aggregators and index surveys (Hectelion Valuation Multiples France/Switzerland 2026, Argos Index mid-market, Damodaran industry data Jan 2026); they are not live quotes, not audited, and not investment advice. Figures are order-of-magnitude and should be validated by a professional before any client-facing use. A `default` set is used when the sector is unmatched.

peers.name: Mid-cap SaaS (Euronext Growth composite)
peers.note: Recurring-revenue software profile; upper end reflects growth premium.

peers.name: IT services mid-cap (composite)
peers.note: Services mix lowers the revenue multiple vs pure SaaS.

peers.name: Digital platform (composite)
peers.note: Scalable platform economics; high multiple, growth-dependent.

peers.name: Precision manufacturing (composite)
peers.note: Capital-intensive; multiples anchored to the Argos mid-market median.

peers.name: Industrial equipment mid-cap (composite)
peers.note: Order-book visibility supports the upper band.

peers.name: Medtech mid-cap (composite)
peers.note: Regulatory moat and margins lift the multiple.

peers.name: Consumer brand mid-cap (composite)
peers.note: Brand strength drives the spread within the band.

peers.name: Specialty retail (composite)
peers.note: Thin margins keep multiples toward the low end.

peers.name: Beverage mid-cap (composite)
peers.note: Stable cash flows; multiple near the sector median.

peers.name: Renewables developer (composite)
peers.note: Pipeline and policy tailwinds support the upper band; low-confidence sector.

peers.name: Business-services mid-cap (composite)
peers.note: Asset-light; multiple near the sector median. Low-confidence sector.

peers.name: General mid-market (Argos Index)
peers.note: Argos Index / Epsilon Research eurozone mid-market median (~8.3x EBITDA, Q4 2025) — the fallback when the sector is unmatched.
